human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for facilities-specific vendor-management behavior, permit and inspection dependencies, outage and return-to-service boundaries, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to facilities vendor readiness, supply continuity, outage-aware maintenance status, and return-to-service escalation rather than generic purchasing or contract administration.

Authority sources cover OSHA construction safety and storage duties, GSA contract administration and facilities maintenance standards, official permit and inspection workflow guidance, and federal submittal and closeout references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, permit gaps, shortages, vendor blockers, approval-control bypass requests, substitution pressure, incomplete inventory evidence, inspection and closeout blockers, safety-driven escalation, adjacent-lane rejection, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, and tenant-specific terminology normalization.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/facilities-services-and-building-operations/vendor-management-facilities-specialist/ so it remains distinct from project controls, permitting, inspection, safety, superintendent, facilities maintenance leadership, and licensed-trade lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated facilities-services-and-building-operations vendor-management specialist for availability assessment, vendor constraint handling, shortage escalation, permit and inspection-aware maintenance workflow support, and return-to-service boundary enforcement.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
